highlights: Preview deadline extended; small quality-of-life updates while native apps are in focus.

changes.text: Experimental API and MCP port settings are now exposed. Stability is not guaranteed; expect changes.
Inline marketplace may still render a blank or garbled page in certain cases.
Inspector cannot trigger tool calls yet; capability diagnostics remain limited.
This build primarily extends the preview countdown while we focus efforts on native app development.

highlights: First public preview of the MCPMate desktop app for macOS, debuting the dashboard, profiles, clients, servers, market, runtime, and settings pillars needed for day-to-day MCP operations.

changes.text: Dashboard delivers live status cards plus CPU / memory timelines to monitor MCPMate and host usage.
Profiles panel enables composing server/tool bundles and applying them to hosted clients for in-place switching.
Marketplace supports browsing the built-in catalog, drag-and-drop bundle imports, and pulling configurations from existing clients.
Improved client detection refresh and stabilized marketplace install flows to reduce first-run friction.
Settings highlights preview WIP controls (Application Mode, language selector, Dock/Menu Bar toggles) that are still evolving.
Runtime page provides one-click uv / Bun installation and repair for environments where servers fail to start.
Hosted client mode supports in-place profile switching; transparent mode only writes configuration files.
API / MCP port configuration is not available yet. Developers running multiple instances may encounter conflicts; the next release will add custom port settings.
The inline marketplace can occasionally render a blank or garbled view. An upcoming upgrade will address these display issues.
Inspector currently cannot trigger tool calls. A fix is in progress to restore capability diagnostics.
